content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 17, 1991


Brent D. Boger
PARICHAN, PENBERG, CROSSMAN & HARVEY
2350 West Shaw, Suite 130
Fresno, CA 93711-3412

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-91-291

Dear Mr. Boger:

This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the duties and responsibilities of Robert C. Smith under
the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Your
request does not address a pending decision but rather seeks
general guidance. Accordingly, we treat your letter as a request
for informal assistance under the provisions of Regulation 18329
(copy enclosed).2 Our advice is limited to the provisions of the
Act. Please consult the city attorney for advice regarding the
provisions of the City of Fresno campaign control ordinance.

QUESTION

May a councilmember use campaign funds to fund the operation
of a district office and to provide constituent services?

CONCLUSION

A councilmember may use campaign funds to fund the operation
of a district office and to provide constituent services so long
as these activities are directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-91-291
Page 2


FACTS³

On May 7, 1991, Robert C. Smith was elected to a four-year
term on the Fresno City Council. Councilmember Smith assumed
office on May 14, 1991. Councilmember Smith has no significant
amount of campaign funds remaining in his account. He plans to
raise campaign funds both before and after the end of this fiscal
year.

In the city of Fresno, one city employee is assigned to each
councilmember for the conduct of city business. However,
Councilmember Smith wishes to use campaign funds to fund the
operation of a district office which would be engaged in political
activities and provide constituent services. Councilmember Smith
intends to hire one or more employees who will be engaged in
fundraising activities, conduct research on city issues, and deal
with citizen complaints. At this time Councilmember Smith does
not contemplate the purchase of equipment or supplies for this
office which will be equipped with items which have been donated
to the councilmember. For purposes of our analysis, we assume
that no public funds will be used for the district office.

You represent Councilmember Smith and seek our advice on his
behalf. You have enclosed for our consideration a copy of Article
22 of the Municipal Code of the City of Fresno which contains the
campaign ordinances of the city.

ANALYSIS

The general rule is that any expenditure of campaign funds
must be, at a minimum, reasonably related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 89512).⁴

You have stated that Councilmember Smith intends to open a
district office. Campaign funds may not be used to purchase real
property. However, campaign funds may be used to lease real
property for up to one year at a time where the use of that
property is directly related to political, legislative, or
governmental purposes. Real property is deemed directly related
to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose, so long as
its use for other purposes is only incidental to its use for
political, legislative, or governmental purposes. Legal title

_______________
³ These facts contain information you provided to me during the
course of our telephone conversation of June 10, 1991.

⁴ Where an expenditure confers a substantial personal benefit on
the candidate or officeholder, the expenditure must be directly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. A
substantial personal benefit is defined as a direct personal
benefit to the candidate or officeholder of $100 or more.
(Section 89511(b)(2).)

Our File No. I-91-291
Page 3

must not reside, in whole or in part, in the candidate, elected
officer, member of his or her immediate family, or the treasurer
of the committee. (Section 89517.)

Councilmember Smith intends to use his district office to
continue to raise funds for his candidacy. This use of the office
is directly related to a political purpose. Additionally,
Councilmember Smith intends to use the office to conduct research
on city matters and to handle citizen complaints. These uses are
directly related to political, governmental and legislative
purposes. Accordingly, Councilmember Smith may use campaign funds
to lease an office for these purposes.

Section 89513(b) further provides:

(b) Campaign funds shall not be used to pay
for or reimburse the cost of professional services
unless the services are directly related to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

(1) Expenditures by a campaign committee to
pay for professional services reasonably required
by the campaign committee to assist it in the
performance of its administrative functions are
directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose.

Section 89513(b).

As discussed above, Councilmember Smith wishes to hire one or
more employees to provide services such as research on city issues
and constituent relations. These services are directly related to
a political, legislative or governmental purpose. Additionally,
these employees would be engaged in fundraising. This activity is
directly related to a political purpose. Accordingly, the use of
campaign funds to pay the salary of employees engaged in such
activities is permissible under the provisions of the Act.
(Hunter Advice Letter, No. I-91-247, copy enclosed.)

You have advised us that at this time Councilmember Smith
does not intend to purchase furnishings for his office but rather
intends to use equipment which has been donated to him. To
provide you with guidance in the event Councilmember Smith should
decide, at a later date, to make additional expenses in connection
with this office, we are enclosing the Viramontes Advice Letter,
No. A-90-0515 and the Connelly Advice Letter, No. I-91-123.

___________

5 Please note that the statutory provisions cited in this letter
have been renumbered. The use of campaign funds is now governed
by Sections 89510 through 89521.

Our File No. I-91-291
Page 4


We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]
By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:dg

Enclosures

5 Please note that the statutory provisions cited in this letter have been renumbered. The use of campaign funds is now governed by Sections 89510 through 89521.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry. Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.
